summary: Problem 8 is an open research-level conjecture posed by Mohammed Abouzaid asking whether a combinatorial condition (exactly 4 Lagrangian faces per vertex) on polyhedral Lagrangian surfaces in R^4 forces the existence of a Lagrangian smoothing. With a 9% solvability score and no substantive proof attempt on record, the single tracked issue is merely a seed/placeholder rather than a real proof to evaluate. There is effectively no candidate proof; the immediate need is foundational groundwork rather than error correction.
problems: No actual proof attempt exists — the 'best proof' entry shows 100 open issues and the lone closed issue was a placeholder seed task, not a mathematical submission.
Core mathematical blocker: no constructive mechanism is established to produce a Hamiltonian isotopy K_t smoothing the polyhedral corners while preserving the Lagrangian condition near each vertex.

The combinatorial hypothesis (exactly 4 faces per vertex) has not been connected to any local model that guarantees a smoothable Lagrangian neighborhood; the link between local vertex geometry and global existence is unproven.
Status reporting is inconsistent (best proof shows '100 open issues' while issue tracker lists 1 resolved placeholder), making it unclear what work, if any, has been seriously attempted.
Very low solvability (9%) reflects that this is genuinely open research with no known full solution, so 'completing a proof' may be infeasible short of original research.
highlights: The problem is precisely and rigorously stated, with clear definitions of polyhedral Lagrangian surface, Lagrangian smoothing, and the key 4-faces-per-vertex condition.
The placeholder issue was correctly triaged and closed as having no proof content, keeping the tracker clean of false positives.
The local-to-global structure (analyze each vertex link, then assemble) provides a natural and tractable decomposition for any future attempt.

suggested_todos.title: Document a precise local model for a 4-face vertex link and determine whether it admits a Lagrangian smoothing in a neighborhood
Survey existing literature on Lagrangian smoothings/desingularizations (e.g., Lagrangian surgery, Joyce, Lawlor necks) and record applicable techniques in an issue
Reconcile the inconsistent status metadata (100 open issues vs. single resolved placeholder) and establish an accurate proof-state record
Construct explicit examples of 4-face-per-vertex polyhedral Lagrangians and test smoothability computationally or by hand to gather evidence for/against the conjecture
Draft a formal statement separating the local existence lemma from the global Hamiltonian isotopy assembly to scope partial-progress targets
